allowed_work: Inspect local PKCS#7, P7B or P7C certificate-bundle artifacts as metadata-only evidence.
Check local parser availability through Windows certutil.exe or OpenSSL.
Record file size, extension, hashes and parser return status.
Record certificate-count signals where the local parser exposes them without storing certificate values.
Flag PFX, PKCS#12, private-key and signing requests as out of scope.
prohibited_work: Store passwords, PINs, private keys, certificate material, session cookies, one-time codes or encrypted key blobs in Git.
Import PFX or PKCS#12 software-token files.
Load private keys or request PINs.
Upload certificate material, client data or mandate content to an external service or LLM.
Treat certificate-bundle evidence as proof of notarial signature readiness.
day0: Confirm the source artifact is a certificate bundle, not a software token.
day1: Run scripts/inspect_certbundle.py against the local PKCS#7/P7B/P7C artifact.
day2: Re-run the evidence check after certificate-chain, workstation or parser changes.
required_accounts_or_approvals: Security review before any scope expansion
